title: Global Economy Faces Hard Times as Conflict in Iran Continues
subtitle: Rising oil prices and supply chain problems affect businesses and nations around the world.

summary.en: A conflict between the US and Iran has led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global energy. This situation has caused oil prices to rise, affecting airlines and major car manufacturers like Toyota. Southeast Asian nations are now working together to create a fuel-sharing plan to manage the energy crisis. While US President Donald Trump claims that negotiations are ongoing, the situation remains unstable. Experts warn that the economic damage from these disruptions will likely last for years.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗之間的衝突導致全球能源關鍵要道荷莫茲海峽遭到封鎖。此局勢推升了油價，影響了航空公司與豐田等大型汽車製造商。東南亞國家目前正合作制定燃料共享計畫以應對能源危機。儘管美國總統川普聲稱談判仍在進行，但局勢依然不穩。專家警告，這些中斷所造成的經濟損害恐將持續數年。

paragraphs.en: The world economy is facing serious challenges as the conflict between the United States and Iran continues. A major point of tension is the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway that is essential for global trade. According to the BBC, more than one-fifth of the world’s oil and gas usually passes through this area. Since the start of the war between the US-Israel alliance and Iran, the strait has been effectively blocked, causing a global energy crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著美國與伊朗之間的衝突持續，全球經濟正面臨嚴峻挑戰。荷莫茲海峽是全球貿易的關鍵要道，也是緊張局勢的主要焦點。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，全球超過五分之一的石油與天然氣通常會經過此區域。自從美國與以色列聯盟和伊朗之間的戰爭爆發以來，該海峽實際上已被封鎖，導致全球能源危機。

paragraphs.en: On Friday, the situation became more tense when the US and Iran exchanged fire in the area. The US military stated that it performed self-defense strikes after Iranian attacks on US ships. However, Iranian state media claimed that the US violated a ceasefire—an agreement to stop fighting—that was established in April. While US President Donald Trump stated that the ceasefire is still in place, the event caused the price of Brent oil to rise by nearly 3% to about $100 a barrel. Before the conflict began, oil was trading at approximately $70 a barrel.
paragraphs.zh: 週五，隨著美伊兩國在該地區交火，局勢變得更加緊張。美軍表示，在伊朗攻擊美國船隻後，他們採取了自衛性打擊。然而，伊朗官媒聲稱美國違反了四月份達成的停火協議。儘管美國總統川普（Donald Trump）表示停火協議依然有效，但此事件導致布蘭特原油價格上漲近 3%，來到每桶約 100 美元。在衝突爆發前，油價約為每桶 70 美元。

paragraphs.en: The impact of this conflict is spreading across many industries. Airlines are feeling the pressure as the cost of jet fuel has jumped by about 50%. The owner of British Airways, IAG, reported that it expects its fuel costs to reach €9 billion this year. This is about €2 billion higher than last year. Chris Beauchamp, an analyst at the investment platform IG, noted that market confidence remains low because a full resolution to the conflict still seems far away.
paragraphs.zh: 這場衝突的影響正蔓延至許多產業。由於航空燃油成本飆升約 50%，航空公司正承受巨大壓力。英國航空（British Airways）的母公司國際航空集團（IAG）表示，預計今年燃油成本將達到 90 億歐元，比去年高出約 20 億歐元。投資平台 IG 的分析師畢徹姆（Chris Beauchamp）指出，由於衝突似乎還遠未完全解決，市場信心依然低迷。

paragraphs.en: Large manufacturers are also suffering. Toyota, the world’s biggest carmaker, reported a £3 billion loss linked to the war. The company explained that the prices of parts and materials have soared, and sales have dropped. According to The Guardian, the Japanese manufacturer faced a massive increase in material costs, while also losing money due to lower sales. Takanori Azuma, a chief officer at Toyota, stated that the company cannot fully cover the negative impact of the conflict, which affects everything from transportation expenses to the cost of paint used in factories.
paragraphs.zh: 大型製造商也深受其害。全球最大的汽車製造商豐田（Toyota）報告稱，與戰爭相關的損失達 30 億英鎊。該公司解釋，零件與原物料價格飆漲，且銷售額下滑。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，這家日本製造商面臨材料成本大幅增加，同時也因銷售疲軟而虧損。豐田首席官員東尚典（Takanori Azuma）表示，公司無法完全抵銷衝突帶來的負面影響，這些影響涵蓋了從運輸費用到工廠塗料成本等各個層面。

paragraphs.en: Countries are now looking for ways to protect their economies. Leaders from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) met in the Philippines to discuss the crisis. Because the region imports more than half of its crude oil from the Middle East, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has been very damaging. At the summit, ASEAN leaders agreed to create a regional fuel-sharing plan. They also discussed building a regional power grid and fuel stockpile to reduce their dependence on energy from the Middle East in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 各國目前正尋求保護自身經濟的方法。東南亞國家協會（ASEAN）的領袖在菲律賓會面，討論這場危機。由於該地區超過一半的原油仰賴中東進口，荷莫茲海峽的封鎖造成了嚴重損害。在峰會上，東協領袖同意制定一項區域燃料共享計畫。他們也討論了建立區域電網與燃料儲備，以減少未來對中東能源的依賴。

paragraphs.en: However, finding a solution will not be easy. Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. admitted that the practical details of the fuel-sharing plan still need to be clarified. He warned that the economic consequences of the war will be felt for a long time, noting that even a few weeks of disruption will take years to fix. In late March, the Philippines became the first country to declare a national emergency due to low energy supplies.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，尋求解決方案並不容易。菲律賓總統小馬可仕（Ferdinand Marcos Jr.）坦言，燃料共享計畫的具體執行細節仍需釐清。他警告，戰爭帶來的經濟後果將持續很長一段時間，並指出即使只是幾週的中斷，也需要數年時間才能修復。三月下旬，菲律賓成為第一個因能源供應短缺而宣布進入國家緊急狀態的國家。

paragraphs.en: Despite the ongoing tension, President Trump has said that negotiations with Iran are moving forward. He repeated the demand that Tehran must not develop a nuclear weapon, warning that there would be "a lot of pain" if a deal is not signed. Meanwhile, Iranian state media has reported that the situation is back to normal. However, traders remain cautious. Huifeng Chang, an economics researcher at the National University of Singapore, told the BBC that traders view the current ceasefire as fragile. As long as the conflict continues, businesses and governments around the world will likely continue to face high costs and uncertainty.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管緊張局勢持續，川普總統表示與伊朗的談判正在推進。他重申伊朗不得發展核武的要求，並警告若未簽署協議將會面臨「巨大的痛苦」。與此同時，伊朗官媒報導稱局勢已恢復正常。然而，交易員仍持謹慎態度。新加坡國立大學的經濟研究員張慧豐（Huifeng Chang）向 BBC 表示，交易員認為目前的停火協議相當脆弱。只要衝突持續，全球企業與政府很可能將繼續面臨高昂成本與不確定性。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To give and receive something at the same time; here, it refers to military forces firing at each other.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 交換；這裡指雙方互相開火。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於交換禮物或意見，這裡指軍事上的交火。文中：On Friday, the situation became more tense when the US and Iran exchanged fire in the area.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To rise very quickly to a high level.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常用於形容鳥類飛翔，這裡形容價格飆升。文中：The company explained that the prices of parts and materials have soared, and sales have dropped.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To provide enough money to pay for something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（封面）或動詞（覆蓋），這裡指負擔開銷。文中：Takanori Azuma, a chief officer at Toyota, stated that the company cannot fully cover the negative impact of the conflict, which affects everything from transportation expenses to the cost of paint used in factories.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Easily broken, damaged, or likely to fail.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常用於形容玻璃等易碎物品，這裡形容停火協議極易被打破。文中：Huifeng Chang, an economics researcher at the National University of Singapore, told the BBC that traders view the current ceasefire as fragile.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: ASEAN leaders adopt measures to ease economic pain caused by Iran war

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Iran war costs Toyota £3bn as prices of materials soar and sales fall

quiz.question: What specific financial impact has the current conflict had on Toyota according to the article?

options.A: It has suffered a loss of €2 billion due to rising fuel costs.
options.B: It has experienced a £3 billion loss caused by increased material costs and reduced sales.
options.C: It has seen a 50% increase in the cost of paint used for its vehicles.
options.D: It has been forced to close its factories due to a lack of energy supplies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，Toyota 報告了 30 億英鎊的損失，並明確指出這是由於零件與材料價格飆升以及銷售量下滑所導致。選項 A 混淆了 IAG（英國航空母公司）的數據；選項 C 雖然提到油漆成本，但文章並未指出其漲幅為 50%；選項 D 雖提及能源供應問題，但 Toyota 的損失主因並非工廠關閉，而是成本與銷售壓力。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the effectiveness of the current ceasefire mentioned in the article?

options.A: It has successfully stabilized the price of Brent oil at $70 a barrel.
options.B: It is widely trusted by international traders to prevent further conflict.
options.C: It is viewed as unstable and insufficient to eliminate market uncertainty.
options.D: It has been officially canceled by the US government after the recent exchange of fire.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章末段，雖然伊朗媒體稱局勢恢復正常，但研究人員 Huifeng Chang 指出交易員認為停火協議非常「脆弱」（fragile），且只要衝突持續，市場仍將面臨高成本與不確定性。選項 A 錯誤，因為油價已漲至 100 美元；選項 B 與文中提到的交易員保持謹慎相矛盾；選項 D 錯誤，因為川普總統仍表示停火協議依然有效。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'fragile' most closely mean in the context of the ceasefire?

options.A: Easily broken or likely to fail.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到交易員認為停火協議是「fragile」，結合前後文提到雙方發生交火、局勢緊張且未來充滿不確定性，可知此處意指該協議非常脆弱、容易破裂。選項 B 是該詞的字面意思，但在政治語境下不適用；選項 C 與 D 皆與協議的穩定性無關，且不符合文中對市場擔憂的描述。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the US-Iran conflict?

options.A: The conflict is primarily limited to the military sector and has little effect on civilians.
options.B: ASEAN countries have successfully solved the energy crisis through new trade agreements.
options.C: The conflict has created widespread global economic instability and rising costs across multiple sectors.
options.D: The US and Iran are close to signing a permanent peace treaty to lower oil prices.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章全面探討了衝突對全球經濟的影響，包括油價上漲、航空公司燃料成本增加、製造商損失，以及各國為應對能源危機所做的努力，顯示該衝突對全球各產業造成了廣泛的負面衝擊。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 過於樂觀，文中提到細節尚未明確；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中提到協議仍不穩定，且川普總統警告若不簽署協議將有「痛苦」的後果。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
